description: secretx-k8s/src/lib.rs:69. parsed.param('key').map(str::to_owned) accepts ?key= (empty value) without validation. On get, data.get("") returns None and we surface NotFound — confusing: caller sees NotFound for a Secret that exists. On put, BTreeMap key is empty string; the K8s apiserver rejects empty data keys (must match [-._a-zA-Z0-9]+) and we surface a Backend error with an opaque server message. Fix: in from_uri, after extracting key, if key.is_some_and(|k| k.is_empty() || !valid_secret_key(k)) return Err(InvalidUri('?key= must be non-empty and match [-._a-zA-Z0-9]+')). The validation belongs at construction time so callers get the failure immediately, not at first I/O.
title: Empty ?key= value silently accepted, results in NotFound or invalid POST
